text: @NoxiousNinja I think this is correct. Light perception is logarithmic, so to perceive something twice as bright, you need to quadruple the power. That would suggest that privacy mode actually DRAINS more battery even though you're turning off half the LEDs. The remaining half of the LEDs that are on need to be MORE than twice as bright as they were before to maintain the same level of brightness. But, Samsung probably knows this, and has made the tradeoff that turning on privacy mode slightly DECREASES your brightness, so that overall power consumption is roughly the same, assuming you don't adjust your brightness settings after turning it on.
